all set to recommence the meal plan, is doing her shopping tomorrow, and is ready to get started again. She plans to begin walking regularly again tomoorow and already has a home workout programme.She will complete her measurements on Saturday. goal is to lose 10kg in preparation for her other daughter’s wedding. confirmed that she will check in with me and respond to my messages

Suzy is feeling stuck and frustrated. She has had no weight change for a few weeks despite maintaining good nutrition.
She is currently waiting to see her immunologist, who is considering prescribing Mounjaro to assist with weight loss and support her thyroid condition  and prevent diabetes.

Her surgeon has now given her the all-clear to slowly increase her step count, and she is continuing to work with her physiotherapist. We discussed focusing on upper body, core, and back exercises for now.
I have asked Suzy to send through the advice from her physio regarding any precautions or movement restrictions. She should still be able to complete exercises that do not impact her knee.could troy assist with some exercises.
Suzy also has a mini pedal machine that she used prior to surgery, and I encouraged her to start using this again gradually.
She is experiencing some classic symptoms of perimenopause and is commencing HRT tonight.
We also discussed the importance of recognising the positive things she is achieving rather than focusing only on what she feels she is not doing. I have asked her to write a list of her achievements and post it somewhere visible so she can acknowledge and feel proud of her progress.
I encouraged Suzy to reach out again after her upcoming appointments if she feels she needs another chat and that supports will continue to support her

Laurie is extremely fixated on the number on the scales. When she joined the BBA, her sole stated goal was to reach 72 kg before an event on 11 June. This target weight was driven by her desire to run with her dog without knee pain.
Currently, Laurie is able to run with her dog and reports no knee pain. Her jeans fit comfortably; she has had to purchase new undies as her previous ones are now too big. Her bra fits better, and measurements and progress photos show marked changes. There have been several other positive outcomes as well.
Despite these improvements, Laurie is struggling to move past the number on the scale. I have asked her to have her husband put the scales away and to write a list of all the positive changes she has achieved and display it somewhere visible.

Gae is feeling much better after our chat and has decided to give this another go rather than giving up. We discussed consistency over perfection, along with some simple strategies to reduce alcohol intake and increase daily water intake.
She is happy with her current step count and has a walking buddy, which is helping her stay consistent. She is not currently doing structured workouts.
Gae has had left shoulder surgery with complications, which is now healed, and is awaiting right shoulder surgery. With this in mind, I’ve suggested keeping things simple with a body‑weight programme and a 15‑minute workout that avoids shoulder loading.
She is happy to reach out for support and will be more involved in the group.
Could you please send her 
Body‑Weight and 15 minute Programme
05/13 perimenopause symptoms, unclear GP, 10,000 steps + 3x gym, MLB meet up

Daniela is doing well and is noticing positive changes since starting BBA.
Currently, her biggest challenge is fatigue. This is not surprising, as her children are not sleeping well for a number of reasons, and she is averaging around 4–5 hours of sleep per night.
We discussed perimenopause and HRT in detail. We also agreed on setting a bare minimum, non-negotiable routine for the week. Daniela is happy with:
•7,000 steps daily
•1 workout per week
•Continuing with her nutrition for the next 2–3 weeks until things settle at home
I have encouraged her to try psyllium or chia seeds to help with constipation.
She is currently waiting for an appointment with a women’s health clinic to review her progesterone dosing. She believes this may be contributing to her nausea.
Daniela remains very active in the community and is engaged in coaching calls.

Kim has had a very postiive experience with BBA. While she has not yet reached her goal, she feels she has learned enough to continue independently.
Her decision to leave is entirely due to financial constraints, as she is unable to maintain the commitment at this time.
Kim spoke very positively about her experience overall.
The strong sense of community
The support provided by the coaches and support team
The ease of use of the app
She did not express any negative feedback.
And wishes she could stay

Michelle is feeling frustrated and overwhelmed, though not with anything I felt I could directly assist with at this stage.
She is having an ongoing battle with her IBS. We discussed a few options, but she has a very good insight and understanding of her condition, including the dos and don’ts. She has undergone multiple investigations without receiving a clear diagnosis.
I suggested she listen to the podcast with Dr. Kirsten Green and explore her website for additional perspectives.
Michelle does not want to return to the gym at this stage. However, due to her back injury, she will continue with rehab Pilates twice per week.
She has been averaging around 7,000 steps over the past few weeks but cannot increase this due to back pain. I suggested she maintain this level and reset her watch goal to 7,000 steps so she isn’t feeling disappointed or discouraged each day.
She has been doing some reading and believes she cannot fast due to perimenopause. To my knowledge, perimenopause is not a strict contraindication to fasting; please correct me if I am wrong 
I also suggested she write a list of the positive things she has achieved recently and share them with the group to help shift focus and build momentum.
She is planning on meeting up with another BBA memwber who lives close by for support and accountablitity
tressed - plateauing //  6-8 travel to SG //  NA drink 2 wks nightly + post

think she was a good celebration call target. She also has a few people in mind, that would benefit, but she doesnt want to approach them. She wants them so see her transformation and then talk about the academy.. Feel like this could be a bit of a direction the good calls could go in.

was ok shes got good progress for the 3 weeks shes with us, but far from confident that this will work yet. Still a lot of self doubt. Same opinion as above. I think its just to early for the result we are trying to achieve.

Donelda shared that she’s currently feeling like she’s failing, particularly around nutrition. She identified a pattern of self-sabotage with chocolate—once she has one piece, she feels like she’s “blown it” and continues eating beyond what she intended.
We discussed some practical strategies to help break that cycle:
•Pre-portioning chocolate (e.g. individually wrapping a few pieces rather than sitting with a full block)
•Allowing herself a small portion daily instead of restriction
•Introducing a 30-second pause before going for an extra piece, to help create awareness and reduce automatic behaviour
From a nutrition standpoint, she will continue focusing on improving her habits gradually, with an emphasis on prioritising protein. We agreed that while further changes will be needed over time, the priority right now is building some early wins and consistency.
Goals for the Week:
•3 × 15-minute workouts
•Daily step goal: 6,500 steps
I’ve also asked Donelda to:
•Write a list of positive behaviours and wins
•Keep this visible, and post in the group
5/21 - 5000 steps // 15 min 3x a week gym, home soon // nightly reminder to track

Chelsea is doing okay but quite up and down at the moment.
She has been experiencing some gut issues—unsure whether this is related to gluten or lactose—which has been causing some nausea alongside increased stress levels.
We discussed keeping things simple with protein options. The plan is to choose one brand of protein powders/bars and stick with it, using this as a mini elimination approach to see if symptoms improve.
We also explored whole food alternatives to bars/powders, including deli meats, boiled eggs, and cooked chicken.
A simple plan has been set for the next 7 days:
•Continue prioritising whole foods with a focus on protein
•Aim for 3,000 steps per day (consider getting a tracker instead of relying on phone)
•Complete 2 gym sessions this week
•Make an accountability post and engage more within the group/CC
Chelsea mentioned feeling intimidated by others in the group who are achieving higher step counts, which may be impacting her confidence.
Follow-up booked: 29/05 at 4:30pm
5/ 21 lives in caravan // will log daily // share in group for accountability // cc nest week // follow-up: 29 May

Donelda has had a great week and is so much brighter this week.
She’s been consistently getting her steps in and making positive nutritional choices.
She’s also shown excellent control with chocolate this week — limiting herself to small amounts (half a bar on one occasion and half of a smaller portion twice this week), which is a huge improvement for her.
She hasn’t completed any structured workouts due to a pinched nerve in her neck and is  cautious about aggravating it. I suggested incorporating some simple bodyweight movements into her routine, such as squats and calf raises while cooking dinner. With yhe goal to build her confidence. Then move to
choosinh exercises from her programme that don’t aggravate her neck. Once her confidence improves, we can look at addressing the neck issue with Troy if needed.
She attended a CC session last week, which is great to see. I’ve encouraged her to jump on another one and to stay engaged by posting in the group. She understands that you are her first point of contact and main source of support.

Rebecca shared that she borrowed money from her mother to pay for the BBA program, but didn’t tell her what she used it for. She explained that she felt embarrassed about borrowing the money and, as a result, struggled to properly start the program.
Throughout the call, she provided multiple reasons for why she didn’t follow or engage with the program consistently. She acknowledged that she previously lost weight on Jenny Craig because the meals were prepared for her and required very little effort, but she regained the weight after stopping.
She did recognise that she needs to take ownership of her health and weight loss and was hoping for a light Bilby moment around that She neliebed that she ahas the tools from BBA to carry on with if she needs to
Her feedback was that the program felt too complex for someone in her situation as a busy mum, particularly due to the amount of reading and video content. She expressed a preference for something simpler and more “done-for-you.”
Such a shame she has wasted this chance to try to change her life x

I discussed with her to focus on a substitution for afternoon sugar cravings, protein bar, protein shake etc. plus she wants to start the day with a high protein meal. Please hammer her with this this week a couple times. Ill set another call up with her at the end of this week.
